title: Dactylorhiza × turcestanica
intro: Dactylorhiza × turcestanica is a hybrid orchid species, resulting from the cross between the orchids Dactylorhiza coccinea and Dactylorhiza romana. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza × turcestanica include iridoid glycosides, such as cypellic acid, and other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Dactylorhiza × turcestanica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into cough syrups and respiratory remedies.
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza × turcestanica is a hybrid species of orchid, resulting from a cross between Dactylorhiza romanzoffiana and Dactylorhiza ponzii. This species is classified as a natural hybrid orchid, specifically within the genus Dactylorhiza. The classification of Dactylorhiza × turcestanica is further supported by its distinctive characteristics, which combine features from its parent species. As a hybrid, it is not assigned a separate species classification, but rather a hybrid designation. The classification of Dactylorhiza × turcestanica is primarily based on its botanical and morphological characteristics.

Its roots and leaves contain various bioactive compounds, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
